question: Is the shopping cart now positioned in the front-left corner of the frame?
Does the shopping cart appear closer to the camera and scaled up to approximately 120% of its original size?
Is the shopping cart completely removed from its original mid-right position?
Is the floor in the vacated area seamlessly inpainted, with tile grout lines restored consistently?
Are the shelves and products in the vacated area filled in naturally without visible repetition or tiling artifacts?
Do the wheels of the relocated cart align realistically with the tile grout lines on the floor?
Are floor reflections and shadows updated to correctly match the new position and scale of the cart?
Do the cart wheels make realistic contact with the floor without floating or sinking?
Does the relocated cart avoid implausible intersections with nearby objects, such as cutting into shelves or products?
Is the complex wire mesh structure of the shopping cart preserved without distortion, melting, or broken lines?
Are the proportions of the shopping cart maintained without appearing stretched or squashed?
Is the text on product packaging (on the cart and shelves) sharp and free from warping or smearing?
Is the boundary of the relocated cart seamless, without halos, jagged edges, or floating artifacts?
Is the relocated cart sharp and in focus, consistent with the depth of field of the foreground?
